parsed_review.summary: CP00-294 (RP08 email-DMS-core Hermes slice descriptor pack, Risk B, 40 units, range RP08.P08.M02.S15-RP08.P08.M04.S12) was reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob across the contract, registry/service/validators/index source, tests, the contract validator script, README, manifest, command-evidence, and all review-hardening artifacts. Coverage matches the baseline exactly: M02=6/M03=22/M04=12=40 units; deliverables hermes_evidence=20/implementation=16/test=2/claude_review=2=40; micro-titles 6/22/12; last unit RP08.P08.M04.S12. Continuity is correct (CP293 M02.S14 -> CP294 M02.S15; handoff to CP00-295/RP08.P08.M04.S13, no gap/overlap). Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold across service.js freezeCp294Result, both contract no-write attestations, and the manifest (all write/runtime/dispatch flags false; synthetic_only/no_real_data true; no Hermes runtime receipts, command runtime, state writes, or object storage). Claude remains a read-only reviewer and explicit non-approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval false; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true; manifest claude_not_final_approver true; enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation true). Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic H08 evidence / C08 review-packet handling (receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening is fully encoded via 11 invalid_review_blockers (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims); readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1446 bytes); no pre-existing raw-output.json/review-receipt.json. The committed contract JSON is in sync with the source (validator deepEquals requirements and attestation; all 7 public exports imported and exercised; tests assert coverage/descriptor-only/authority boundaries). production_ready is correctly held at false (status implemented_pending_review) with empty review receipts, so there is no premature production-ready or enterprise-trust claim to refute. Unrelated dirty files (the .docx and the separate-track LDIP c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly excluded; new-session-handoff.md has no system-reminder injection in its current state and is out of pack scope. One P3 informational note only: the harness-generated changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own artifact directory as 'unrelated', a cosmetic labeling overlap that does not affect the authoritative active implementation refs. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; pack and goal closeout are not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own artifact directory as 'unrelated'
findings.details: The review-harness scope artifact lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-294/ (the pack's own manifest/command-evidence directory) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md under both untracked_implementation_files (lines 96-97) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 102-103). The pack manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved more accurately lists only the unrelated .docx and the LDIP file. The authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs correctly contains only the 8 tracked implementation files (contract, README, index/registry/service/validators, test, validator script), so review scope and the production_ready determination are unaffected. This is a cosmetic labeling overlap in a generated artifact, not a defect in the pack's committed deliverables or product code.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the scope generator so the pack's own untracked artifact directory is not also bucketed under 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved', or rename that field to dirty_files_preserved to remove the semantic friction. No change is required for CP00-294 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not block pack or goal closeout. Active implementation refs, coverage, boundary, authority, and review-hardening checks all pass.
